creator: Axel.Rau@Chaos1.DE
text:
Having
  USES pgsql:9.0+
in a port Makefile and some PostgreSQL 9.x installed, say
  postgresql94-client
gives compile errors and confusing error messages like:
- - -
[root@vps: /usr/ports/www/calendarserver] # make
===>  py27-calendarserver-5.1_3 cannot install: the port wants postgresql-client version 9.0 9.1 9.2 9.3 9.4 and you have version 9.4 installed.
*** Error code 1

Stop.
make: stopped in /usr/ports/www/calendarserver
- - -

Everything works if
  USES pgsql:9.0+
is replaced by
  USES pgsql
(reported by w.litter@aon.at in 201613), or something like
  DEFAULT_VERSIONS= pgsql=9.4
in /etc/make.conf
